t: How many lines of symmetry does a square have?<svg width="82" height="82" viewBox="0 0 82 82" style="display:inline-block;vertical-align:middle;margin:0 5px"><rect x="11" y="11" width="60" height="60" fill="#fde8d8" stroke="#e67e22" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="41" y1="4" x2="41" y2="78" stroke="#e74c3c" stroke-width="2" stroke-dasharray="5,3"/><line x1="4" y1="41" x2="78" y2="41" stroke="#e74c3c" stroke-width="2" stroke-dasharray="5,3"/><line x1="7" y1="7" x2="75" y2="75" stroke="#e74c3c" stroke-width="2" stroke-dasharray="5,3"/><line x1="75" y1="7" x2="7" y2="75" stroke="#e74c3c" stroke-width="2" stroke-dasharray="5,3"/></svg>

How many lines of symmetry does a circle have?<svg width="68" height="68" viewBox="0 0 68 68" style="display:inline-block;vertical-align:middle;margin:0 5px"><line x1="65.0" y1="34.0" x2="3.0" y2="34.0" stroke="#e74c3c" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="4,3" opacity="0.7"/><line x1="55.9" y1="55.9" x2="12.1" y2="12.1" stroke="#e74c3c" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="4,3" opacity="0.7"/><line x1="34.0" y1="65.0" x2="34.0" y2="3.0" stroke="#e74c3c" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="4,3" opacity="0.7"/><line x1="12.1" y1="55.9" x2="55.9" y2="12.1" stroke="#e74c3c" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="4,3" opacity="0.7"/><circle cx="34" cy="34" r="29" fill="#dff0f8" stroke="#2980b9" stroke-width="2.5"/><text x="34" y="39" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" fill="#2980b9" font-weight="bold">∞</text></svg>

Does the letter Z have a line of symmetry?<svg width="54" height="64" viewBox="0 0 54 64" style="display:inline-block;vertical-align:middle;margin:0 4px"><text x="27" y="54" text-anchor="middle" font-size="48" font-weight="bold" font-family="Arial,sans-serif" fill="#5d3a8e">Z</text><circle cx="43" cy="12" r="9" fill="none" stroke="#e74c3c" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="36" y1="5" x2="50" y2="19" stroke="#e74c3c" stroke-width="2"/></svg>
e: Z has NO line of symmetry.

Butterfly has which type of symmetry?<svg width="100" height="84" viewBox="0 0 100 84" style="display:inline-block;vertical-align:middle;margin:0 5px"><ellipse cx="25" cy="28" rx="23" ry="17" fill="#d7bde2" stroke="#6c3483" stroke-width="2" transform="rotate(-18 25 28)"/><ellipse cx="75" cy="28" rx="23" ry="17" fill="#d7bde2" stroke="#6c3483" stroke-width="2" transform="rotate(18 75 28)"/><ellipse cx="22" cy="57" rx="17" ry="12" fill="#c39bd3" stroke="#6c3483" stroke-width="2" transform="rotate(12 22 57)"/><ellipse cx="78" cy="57" rx="17" ry="12" fill="#c39bd3" stroke="#6c3483" stroke-width="2" transform="rotate(-12 78 57)"/><ellipse cx="50" cy="42" rx="5" ry="27" fill="#6c3483"/><line x1="50" y1="16" x2="39" y2="4" stroke="#6c3483" stroke-width="1.5"/><line x1="50" y1="16" x2="61" y2="4" stroke="#6c3483" stroke-width="1.5"/><circle cx="39" cy="4" r="2.5" fill="#8e44ad"/><circle cx="61" cy="4" r="2.5" fill="#8e44ad"/><line x1="50" y1="1" x2="50" y2="82" stroke="#27ae60" stroke-width="2" stroke-dasharray="5,3"/></svg>
e: Butterfly has 1 vertical line of symmetry.

t: Does the letter S have a line of symmetry?<svg width="54" height="64" viewBox="0 0 54 64" style="display:inline-block;vertical-align:middle;margin:0 4px"><text x="27" y="54" text-anchor="middle" font-size="48" font-weight="bold" font-family="Arial,sans-serif" fill="#5d3a8e">S</text><circle cx="43" cy="12" r="9" fill="none" stroke="#e74c3c" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="36" y1="5" x2="50" y2="19" stroke="#e74c3c" stroke-width="2"/></svg>
e: S has no line of symmetry (it is NOT symmetric by fold).

t: Which letter has NO symmetry?<svg width="54" height="64" viewBox="0 0 54 64" style="display:inline-block;vertical-align:middle;margin:0 4px"><text x="27" y="54" text-anchor="middle" font-size="48" font-weight="bold" font-family="Arial,sans-serif" fill="#5d3a8e">F</text><circle cx="43" cy="12" r="9" fill="none" stroke="#e74c3c" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="36" y1="5" x2="50" y2="19" stroke="#e74c3c" stroke-width="2"/></svg>
e: F has no line of symmetry.

t: Can a shape have more than one line of symmetry?
e: Yes! Square has 4, circle has unlimited.

How many lines of symmetry does the letter X have?<svg width="54" height="64" viewBox="0 0 54 64" style="display:inline-block;vertical-align:middle;margin:0 4px"><text x="27" y="54" text-anchor="middle" font-size="48" font-weight="bold" font-family="Arial,sans-serif" fill="#5d3a8e">X</text><line x1="27" y1="2" x2="27" y2="62" stroke="#27ae60" stroke-width="2" stroke-dasharray="5,3"/><line x1="2" y1="32" x2="52" y2="32" stroke="#27ae60" stroke-width="2" stroke-dasharray="5,3"/></svg>
e: X has 2 lines of symmetry: vertical and horizontal.

t: How many lines of symmetry does a regular pentagon have?<svg width="70" height="70" viewBox="0 0 70 70" style="display:inline-block;vertical-align:middle;margin:0 5px"><polygon points="35.0,6.0 62.6,26.0 52.0,58.5 18.0,58.5 7.4,26.0" fill="#fde8d8" stroke="#e67e22" stroke-width="2.5"/></svg>
Which number has vertical symmetry?
e: 8 has a vertical line of symmetry.

How many lines of symmetry does an arrow pointing to the right have?

e: Isosceles triangle has 1 line of symmetry.

e: Heart has 1 vertical line of symmetry.
